executive_summary: The global network is experiencing elevated systemic friction driven by acute energy volatility in the Middle East and disproportionate psychological anxiety in the Taiwan Strait. While physical supply lines and fuel reserves remain temporarily stable, the confluence of speculative artificial intelligence valuation and widening wealth inequality increases vulnerability to sudden market realignments. Consequently, strategic positioning requires prioritised monitoring of capital flows and localised panic indicators to preempt cascading disruption.
correlations: The extreme volatility in Middle East energy directly feeds into the elevated supply chain stress score, demonstrating that pricing uncertainty translates into logistical precautionary friction before physical shortages manifest.
